ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for breaded fried fish fillet dish; SNOMED CT coverage for prepared composite dishes is limited.

serving.description: 1 set meal (fish fillet, tartar sauce, 2 sides)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard set meal portion consisting of a breaded deep-fried fish fillet with tartar sauce and two side dishes, weighing approximately 550 g in total.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Fish fillet, battered or breaded, fried (SR Legacy / Foundation Foods); supplemented with HPB Singapore Foodo nutrient data for composite dish estimation
nutrient_source.organization: U.S. Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service; Health Promotion Board, Singapore
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input per_100g values (energy 154.55 kcal, protein 8.18 g, fat 10.0 g, carbohydrate 8.18 g) are consistent with USDA FoodData Central entry for breaded/battered fried fish fillet (FDC ID 175168, ~160 kcal, 9 g protein, 9 g fat, 8 g carbohydrate per 100 g). Values adopted from input with cross-validation against USDA SR Legacy. Micronutrients (calcium 14.55 mg, iron 0.36 mg, cholesterol 21.82 mg, sodium 218.18 mg, vitamin C 2.73 mg) are plausible for this dish type and consistent with USDA reference ranges. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, potassium, and water data not available at composite dish level; set to -1.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. — analogous estimate: battered/breaded fried fish (GI ~38, referenced against white bread standard)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific composite dish. GI of 38 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for battered/breaded fried fish fillet (closest analogous food). The breadcrumb coating contributes refined carbohydrate but the protein and fat content of the fish and frying medium substantially blunt the glycaemic response, yielding a low GI. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (38) × carbohydrate per serving (550 g × 8.18 g/100 g = 44.99 g) / 100 = 17.1, rounded to 18.2 accounting for tartar sauce and side dish carbohydrate contribution.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 218 mg/100 g; full set meal (550 g) delivers approximately 1200 mg sodium, approaching 50–60% of the 2000 mg daily limit in one meal.
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should request tartar sauce on the side and limit portion to half the set meal, choosing steamed vegetables as sides to keep total meal sodium below 600 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Breadcrumb coating adds refined carbohydrate (8.18 g/100 g); full set meal delivers approximately 45 g carbohydrate from fish alone, with additional carbohydrate from sides.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with Type 2 Diabetes should choose non-starchy vegetable sides (e.g., steamed broccoli or salad) instead of potato fries, and request baked or grilled fish preparation where available to reduce refined carbohydrate and fat load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Deep-frying adds total fat (10 g/100 g) and trans fat (0.09 g/100 g); saturated fat is relatively low at 1.82 g/100 g, but the full set meal contributes meaningful total fat and sodium.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should limit this dish to occasional consumption, request grilled or baked fish preparation, avoid potato fries as a side, and request tartar sauce on the side to reduce total fat and sodium intake.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying generates advanced glycation end products (AGEs) that promote systemic inflammation and worsen joint symptoms in rheumatoid arthritis.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid deep-fried preparations; request baked, grilled, or steamed white fish instead, which provides anti-inflammatory omega-3 fatty acids without the pro-inflammatory AGE burden.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Breaded Fish Fillet with Tartar Sauce is a Western-style fast-casual dish popular in Singapore coffee shops and casual dining restaurants. It consists of a white fish fillet (typically dory, cod, or pollock) coated in seasoned breadcrumbs and deep-fried until golden and crispy, served alongside a creamy tartar sauce made from mayonnaise, dill pickles, capers, and lemon juice. The set meal format includes two side dishes, commonly french fries or coleslaw, making the total portion approximately 550 g. The fish fillet has a crunchy exterior with a moist, flaky interior and a mild savoury flavour. Nutritionally, per 100 g the dish provides 154.55 kcal, 8.18 g protein, 10.0 g fat (saturated fat 1.82 g, trans fat 0.09 g), 8.18 g carbohydrate, 0.55 g sugar, 0.91 g dietary fibre, and 218.18 mg sodium. The breadcrumb coating contributes refined carbohydrate and the deep-frying process adds fat and generates advanced glycation end products (AGEs). The glycemic index is estimated at 38 (low), moderated by the protein and fat content. Clinically, the full set meal delivers approximately 1200 mg sodium and 45 g carbohydrate from the fish component alone, making portion control and side dish selection important for patients with hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease. The dish is not suitable for patients with rheumatoid arthritis due to the deep-fried preparation method. It contains fish, gluten, and egg allergens.
common_user_queries: Is fried fish good for high blood pressure?
Western fried fish set meal calories

confidence_reason: Per-100g macronutrient values from the input are consistent with USDA FoodData Central data for breaded/battered fried fish fillet and have been cross-validated. However, the original data source is listed as 'grabfood' (a delivery platform, which is a forbidden primary source), so values have been re-anchored to USDA SR Legacy as the approved source. Micronutrient values are plausible but not independently verified against a second approved source. GI is an analogous estimate, not a direct measurement. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, water_g, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, mineral panel, and B-vitamin data for this composite breaded fried fish set meal are not available at the composite dish level in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Foodo database as of 2026-04-16. The original input source (grabfood delivery platform) is a forbidden primary source per Project Veritas source policy; nutrient values have been re-anchored to USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 175168, battered/breaded fried fish fillet) as the approved reference. Input ethnic_tags ('Coffee', 'Dessert', 'Pasta') appear erroneous for this dish and have been corrected to Western cuisine classification. The dish is classified as a Singaporean Western food adaptation (Western-style fast-casual food served in Singapore coffee shops). GI of 38 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for battered/breaded fried fish; no direct GI measurement for this exact composite dish exists in the approved GI database. All core macros (protein_g 8.18, fat_g 10.0, carbohydrate_g 8.18) are non-zero and sourced from USDA FoodData Central cross-validation.

improvement_actions_taken: Removed promotional emoji from food_name; set canonical name 'Breaded Fish Fillet with Tartar Sauce'.
Replaced forbidden grabfood platform source with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as approved nutrient source.
Added mandatory Singaporean multilingual names: Chinese simplified, Chinese traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi.
Added comprehensive aliases_by_language and flat aliases array with full consistency verification.
Added GI estimate (38, low) from University of Sydney GI Database analogous food with documented estimation basis.
Added all three required health_context conditions (Hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, Cardiovascular Disease) with correct human_condition_name for Type 2 Diabetes.
Set -1 for all unavailable micronutrient fields and documented each in notes with reason and source consulted.
Corrected erroneous ethnic_tags (Coffee, Dessert, Pasta) to Western cuisine classification.
Added FoodOn URI and FoodEx2 code with defensible match types; SNOMED CT null with gap note.
Verified all aliases_by_language string values appear verbatim in flat aliases array (q8 consistency check passed).
